name: ICMA Sustainability-Linked Bond Principles
description: The Sustainability-Linked Bond Principles (SLBP) are voluntary process guidelines that outline best practices for financial instruments where the financial and/or structural characteristics can vary depending on whether the issuer achieves predefined Sustainability/ESG objectives as measured through predefined Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) against Sustainability Performance Targets (SPTs).
keyDifference: Unlike Green Bonds where proceeds are earmarked for specific green projects, SLBs can use proceeds for general corporate purposes. The sustainability ambition is expressed through forward-looking KPIs and SPTs, with financial consequences if targets are not met.

selectionOfKPIs.name: Selection of Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)
selectionOfKPIs.description: KPIs should be relevant, core and material to the issuer's overall business, and of high strategic significance to the issuer's current and/or future operations.

selectionCriteria.description: Relevant and core to the issuer's overall business, of high strategic significance to current and/or future operations
Measurable or quantifiable on a consistent methodological basis
Able to be verified by an external and independent party
Able to be benchmarked, i.e., as much as possible using an external reference or definitions to facilitate the assessment of the SPT's level of ambition

disclosureRequirements: Definition of each KPI including calculation methodology
Rationale for selection and relevance/materiality to issuer
Historical data (at least 3 years where feasible) for each KPI
Scope/perimeter of KPI (e.g., % of revenue/operations covered)

commonKPIs.name: GHG emissions intensity
commonKPIs.unit: tCO2e / unit of production or revenue

commonKPIs.name: Energy intensity
commonKPIs.unit: MWh / unit of production or revenue

calibrationOfSPTs.name: Calibration of Sustainability Performance Targets (SPTs)
calibrationOfSPTs.description: SPTs should be ambitious and represent a material improvement in the respective KPIs, be beyond a 'business as usual' trajectory, and where possible be compared to a benchmark or an external reference.

calibrationRequirements.description: Must represent a material improvement in the KPI and be beyond 'business as usual' trajectory
Where applicable, benchmarked against recognised science-based scenarios (e.g., SBTi pathways, IEA Net Zero by 2050)
Compared where possible to an external reference: industry average, best-in-class, science-based scenarios, official national/international targets
Aligned with the issuer's overall sustainability/ESG strategy
Must include a target observation date(s) before or coinciding with bond maturity

disclosureRequirements: Target level for each KPI with target observation date(s)
Timeline for target achievement, including intermediate milestones where relevant
Where applicable, illustration of SPT ambition relative to benchmark/science-based pathway
Any recalculation policies or methodology changes
Trigger event(s) for bond characteristics variation
ambitionBenchmarks: Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) approved pathways
National Determined Contributions (NDCs) under Paris Agreement
Sector-specific decarbonisation pathways (e.g., Transition Pathway Initiative)
Industry peer performance (median or top quartile)

bondCharacteristics.name: Bond characteristics
bondCharacteristics.description: The bond documentation should include the financial and/or structural impact of the KPI(s) not meeting or exceeding the predefined SPT(s).

variationMechanisms.description: Most common mechanism. The coupon rate increases (typically 25 bps) if the issuer fails to achieve the SPT by the target observation date.
variationMechanisms.marketStandard: 25 bps is the most common step-up
variationMechanisms.notes: Step-up applies from the first coupon payment date following the target observation date

variationMechanisms.description: The coupon rate decreases if the issuer achieves or exceeds the SPT. Less common, sometimes combined with step-up.
variationMechanisms.notes: Some issuers use a symmetric structure with both step-up (miss) and step-down (achieve)

variationMechanisms.description: A one-time premium payment to bondholders at maturity if SPTs are not met.
Issuer commits to donate a specified amount to a relevant charity or environmental/social cause if SPTs are not met.
variationMechanisms.notes: Emerging mechanism, often combined with coupon step-up

documentationRequirements: Clear definition of trigger event(s) tied to SPT achievement/non-achievement
Specific financial consequences (e.g., step-up amount in bps)
Fallback mechanisms if KPI cannot be calculated
Any provisions for changes to KPI methodology

reporting.name: Reporting
reporting.description: Issuers should publish up-to-date information on KPI performance at least annually and in any case for any target observation date.
requirements: Publish KPI performance at least annually
Include up-to-date information sufficient to allow investors to monitor SPT performance
Report on any material change in KPI methodology or perimeter
Provide any relevant information enabling investors to assess ambition of SPTs
recommendedDisclosures: Any reassessment of KPI scope or methodology
Illustration of performance relative to SPT trajectory
Explanation of key factors contributing to KPI performance
Any updates to issuer's sustainability strategy

verification.name: Verification
verification.description: Issuers should seek independent and external verification of their performance level against each SPT for each KPI at least once a year, and in any case for any date/period relevant for assessing SPT performance leading to a potential adjustment of the SLB financial and/or structural characteristics, by a qualified external reviewer with relevant expertise.
requirements: Independent external verification of KPI performance against SPTs
At minimum: annually and for any SPT observation date
Verification report to be made publicly available
Verifier should be qualified with relevant expertise

preIssuance.name: Pre-issuance external review
preIssuance.description: Issuers are recommended to appoint an external reviewer to confirm alignment of the SLB with the five SLBP core components.

structuralConsiderations.observationDate: Date on which KPI performance is measured against SPT. Typically set 1-2 years before bond maturity to allow time for verification and coupon adjustment.
structuralConsiderations.reportingDate: Date by which verified KPI performance must be published. Typically a number of months after observation date.
structuralConsiderations.stepUpEffectiveDate: First coupon payment date following the reporting date on which SPT non-achievement is confirmed.
structuralConsiderations.multipleKPIs: Where multiple KPIs are used, documentation should specify whether step-up applies per KPI (cumulative) or on a binary basis (any miss triggers full step-up).
structuralConsiderations.refinancing: SLBs can be refinanced into new SLBs, but issuers should consider the implications for SPT continuity and ambition.
